title: Erigeron angustatus
intro: Erigeron angustatus, commonly known as beaked daisy, is a perennial plant belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, Erigeron angustatus has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include essential oils, flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Erigeron angustatus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to soothe coughs, reduce fever, and promote relaxation.
taxonomy: Erigeron angustatus is a member of the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Erigeron, commonly known as fleabanes or daisies. The genus Erigeron is a group of flowering plants that includes around 50 species. These plants are typically annual or perennial herbs native to North America. Within the plant kingdom, the classification of Erigeron angustatus is as follows: kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Asterales, family Asteraceae, genus Erigeron, and species E. angustatus.
uses: Erigeron angustatus has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and respiratory issues. benefits: Erigeron angustatus, also known as the narrow-leaved daisy or Rocky Mountain fleabane, is a plant species that offers various health benefits.
